title: Pane Selector Syntax
text:
Pane selectors MUST be strings conforming to one of the following formats:

| Syntax | Semantics |
|--------|-----------|
| `id:terminal:N` | Terminal pane with numeric ID `N`. |
| `id:plugin:N` | Plugin pane with numeric ID `N`. |
| `terminal:N` | Shorthand for `id:terminal:N`. |
| `plugin:N` | Shorthand for `id:plugin:N`. |
| `focused` | The currently focused pane in the active tab. |
| `title:<substring>` | All panes whose title contains `<substring>` (case-insensitive). |
| `title:/<regex>/` | All panes whose title matches the regex pattern. |
| `cmd:<substring>` | All panes whose running command contains `<substring>` (case-insensitive). |
| `cmd:/<regex>/` | All panes whose running command matches the regex pattern. |
| `tab:N:index:M` | The pane at position `M` in tab `N`, sorted by type (plugin before terminal) then numeric ID ascending. |

`N` and `M` MUST be non-negative integers.

Leading zeros in numeric IDs MUST be accepted (e.g., `id:terminal:007` is equivalent to `id:terminal:7`).

Regex patterns MUST use Rust `regex` crate syntax.

Implementations MUST validate regex patterns at parse time and return `invalid_params` for invalid patterns.

When a selector matches multiple panes and the operation does not support `all`, the plugin MUST return an `ambiguous_match` error.

When a selector matches zero panes, the plugin MUST return a `no_match` error.

The `focused` selector MUST resolve using the current client's pane focus. If the current client's focus cannot be determined, the plugin MUST fall back to the focused terminal pane in the active tab. If no focused terminal pane exists in the active tab, the plugin MUST fall back to any focused pane, preferring terminals over plugins, ordered by tab index then numeric ID.

Rationale: The selector DSL (per [[ADR-0004]]) provides a human-readable, scriptable addressing scheme that avoids forcing users to discover numeric IDs before every operation. Substring matching is case-insensitive for ergonomics (users shouldn't need to know exact casing), while regex is case-sensitive to give precise control. The `tab:N:index:M` sort order (plugin before terminal, then by ID) is deterministic so that positional selectors produce consistent results across invocations. The focused-pane fallback chain handles the edge case where Zellij's `is_current_client` flag is `false` for all clients (observed in single-client sessions queried from background plugins).
